articles.title: China Sees a ‘Giant With a Limp’ as U.S. Drains Weapons on Iran War
articles.summary: America’s ability to deter China in a war over Taiwan is weakened, Chinese analysts say, giving Beijing leverage in an upcoming summit with President Trump.

articles.title: Prices are up, but Mother's Day still means brunch
articles.summary: It's the biggest day for America's restaurants, and one of the biggest for flower sales. Despite anxieties over rising gas prices, people are ready to splurge to celebrate.

articles.title: Spain readies for evacuations as a hantavirus-hit cruise ship heads for Canary Islands
articles.summary: Spanish authorities are preparing to receive more than 140 passengers and crew members on board a hantavirus-stricken cruise ship headed for the Canary Islands.

articles.title: What video got Kash Patel in the headlines this week? The quiz knows
articles.summary: Plus: The Met Gala, GameStop, Canada and Banksy (again!).

articles.title: Trump's Truth Social lays bare narrow obsessions of an extremely online president
articles.summary: The bulk of the president's social media posts don't make news. But taken together they show what's on his mind as he leads the nation through war and domestic turmoil.

articles.title: European countries emerge as NATO leaders as U.S. role recedes
articles.summary: President Trump's decision to leave NATO in the dark before launching strikes on Iran has inflamed tensions and is putting new urgency on rethinking the alliance.

articles.title: With Spirit in liquidation, here's what happens next to its planes
articles.summary: When Spirit Airlines stopped flying last weekend, it left more than 90 planes scattered across the country. Many will go back to the companies that own them, while Spirit tries to monetize the rest.

articles.title: David Attenborough celebrates his 100th birthday
articles.summary: He was born before the Great Depression, came of age in WWII, and is still making wildlife documentaries. Brits call David Attenborough a national hero, as he celebrates his 100th birthday.

articles.title: Morning news brief
articles.summary: U.S. and Iran trade fire amid month-old ceasefire, Tennessee becomes first state to draw new U.S. House map after SCOTUS decision, U.S.'s continued strikes on alleged drug boats raise questions.

articles.title: As war on Ukraine continues, Russia prepares for smaller Victory Day parade
articles.summary: Russia's annual Victory Day parade will be smaller than previous ones as the effects of the war on Ukraine take a toll.

articles.title: Some people in Iran have kept internet access despite government-imposed blackout
articles.summary: Iran is experiencing the longest internet blackout ever recorded with 99% of the population offline. But certain people  — with "white internet" — have stayed connected this entire time.

articles.title: North Korea says it will deploy new artillery guns targeting Seoul
articles.summary: North Korea says it'll deploy new long-range artillery systems capable of striking the capital region of rival South Korea this year and commission its first naval destroyer in coming weeks.

articles.title: How Energy Prices Are Driving Demand for Solar Panels and Heat Pumps
articles.summary: Already having faced an energy crisis five years ago, more European consumers believe they are “one Trump-ignited war away” from crushing costs.

articles.title: U.S. to Review Mexican Consulates After Right-Wing Claims Against Them
articles.summary: The State Department said it would review all Mexican consulates in the United States, following claims in conservative media that the consulates interfere in politics.

articles.title: Health Officials Race to Track Hantavirus Cruise Ship Outbreak but Predict ‘Limited’ Spread
articles.summary: A Dutch flight attendant and French national were among the latest to be tested in connection with a deadly outbreak on a cruise ship. But an analysis in South Africa showed no mutations in viral samp

articles.title: Trade Court Rules Trump’s 10% Global Tariff Is Illegal
articles.summary: A panel of federal judges found that President Trump could not legally impose the tariff on most imports.

articles.title: Trump Reversed Hormuz Plan After Saudis Denied Airspace Access
articles.summary: Saudi Arabia’s refusal of support suggests that President Trump’s unpredictable approach to Iran has strained ties with one of his closest allies in the Middle East.

articles.title: U.S. military says it intercepted Iranian attacks on 3 Navy ships in Strait of Hormuz
articles.summary: The U.S. military also says that it "targeted Iranian military facilities responsible for attacking U.S. forces." The exchange occurred Thursday as U.S. Navy destroyers transited the Strait of Hormuz,

articles.title: Former U.S. analyst, once jailed for spying for Israel, to run for Israeli parliament
articles.summary: Jonathan Pollard, a former U.S. intelligence analyst who served 30 years for spying for Israel, says he will run in the upcoming Israeli elections.

articles.title: U.S. Debt Hits a Concerning Milestone, and Experts Say Trump’s Policies Could Worsen It
articles.summary: The debt is outgrowing the size of America’s economy. The president’s policies could accelerate the country’s fiscal headaches, experts say, unless policymakers intervene.

articles.title: Why Stocks and Bonds Are Responding Differently to the Iran War
articles.summary: Stock investors are betting that companies will make enormous profits, despite the war. But investors in bonds, including U.S. Treasuries, have other concerns.

articles.title: Dominion still has pending lawsuits against election deniers such as Rudy Giuliani and Sidney Powell
